name: Daemon Commit Logic

tasks.title: Refactor autoCommitPartialWork into a general daemon commit function
tasks.description: Refactor autoCommitPartialWork into a general daemon commit function
tasks.body:
Refactor the existing autoCommitPartialWork function in internal/daemon/iteration.go into a more general commitAfterIteration function that handles both success and failure paths.

The new function should:
1. Accept a commit type (success or failure) and the task ID
2. Check the relevant config flag (commit_on_success or commit_on_failure) before proceeding
3. Respect the auto_commit master switch
4. Respect the commit_state flag when staging (if false, do not stage .wolfcastle/)
5. Use appropriate commit messages per the spec:
   - Success: 'wolfcastle: <task-id> complete'
   - Failure: 'wolfcastle: <task-id> partial (attempt <N>)'
6. Skip the commit if the working tree is clean after staging

The existing autoCommitPartialWork call site (failure path, around line 384) should be updated to call the new function. The success path (around lines 274-373) should also call it after state updates.

Validate task ID format before embedding in commit message (existing pattern).
acceptance_criteria: Both success and failure paths call the commit function

tasks.title: Implement staging area preservation
tasks.body:
Implement staging area preservation so the daemon's git add/commit cycle does not clobber the user's manually staged changes.

The spec suggests two approaches:
1. Stash-based: git stash --keep-index before commit, git stash pop after
2. Separate GIT_INDEX_FILE: use a temporary index file for daemon commits, leaving user's index untouched

Evaluate both approaches and choose the more reliable one. The separate GIT_INDEX_FILE approach is likely more robust because stash can conflict with existing stashes or fail on edge cases.

Implementation should wrap the commit function from task-0001: save user index state before staging, restore after commit. If the preservation mechanism fails, log a warning but do not block the commit (the commit itself is more important than preserving staged state).

Write tests that verify:
- User's staged changes survive a daemon commit
- User's unstaged changes are not affected
- The preservation mechanism handles clean working trees gracefully
constraints: Do not break existing autoCommitPartialWork behavior while adding preservation
acceptance_criteria: User staging area is preserved across daemon commits
Tests verify staged changes survive daemon commits

tasks.title: Update iteration tests for new commit behavior
tasks.description: Update iteration tests for new commit behavior
tasks.body:
Update existing tests in the iteration test file to account for the new commit behavior:

1. Tests that previously asserted on agent commit behavior need updating (the agent no longer commits)
2. Add tests for the success-path commit:
   - Verify commit happens after successful task completion when commit_on_success is true
   - Verify no commit when commit_on_success is false
   - Verify no commit when auto_commit is false
3. Update failure-path tests:
   - Verify commit happens when commit_on_failure is true (existing behavior, new config)
   - Verify no commit when commit_on_failure is false
4. Test commit_state flag:
   - When true, .wolfcastle/ is staged
   - When false, only code changes are staged
5. Test clean working tree skip (no empty commits)
6. Verify commit message format matches spec for both paths
acceptance_criteria: All new config flag combinations are tested
Success and failure paths both have commit test coverage

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Daemon Commit Logic is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify success path in iteration.go calls the commit function
Verify staging area preservation does not clobber user's git index
